skill.name: ng-alain-component-development
skill.description: Create Angular enterprise UI components with ng-alain, @delon, and ng-zorro-antd. The skill provides patterns for tables, schema forms, page headers, ACL controls, responsive layouts, modals, drawers, theming, and accessibility.
skill.summary: Build ng-alain and ng-zorro Angular components for enterprise applications.
skill.author: 7Spade

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution and weak-crypto findings are false positives caused by Markdown code fences and ordinary Angular documentation text. The hardcoded URLs are documentation references only, and no prompt-injection, secret access, executable script, or data exfiltration behavior was found. A low-risk concern remains because example handlers log submitted form and record data.

low_findings.title: Example Code Logs User Data
low_findings.description: Several Angular examples print table events, form submissions, form changes, and task data with console.log. This is not malicious, but teams should remove or guard these logs before production use because submitted values may contain sensitive business data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The affected lines directly show console.log calls on events, records, and submitted task data. The risk is low because the file is instructional Markdown and does not execute code itself.

content.value_statement: Enterprise Angular teams need consistent tables, forms, permissions, and layouts across admin applications. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical ng-alain and ng-zorro patterns for production UI work.
actual_capabilities: Creates Simple Table patterns with STColumn definitions, badges, actions, paging, and loading states.
Builds Schema Form examples with SFSchema properties, async select data, validation, and submit handlers.
Applies PageHeader, breadcrumb, modal, drawer, and ReuseTab-style enterprise layout conventions.
Uses @delon ACL examples for permission-gated buttons and programmatic permission checks.
Shows responsive ng-zorro grid layouts, theme variables, dark mode toggles, and accessibility attributes.
limitations: It provides code patterns and guidance, not a complete generated application.
Service implementations, API contracts, and real data models must come from the host project.
Examples include placeholder console logging that should be removed or guarded for production.
It assumes an Angular project already uses ng-alain, @delon packages, and ng-zorro-antd.

use_cases.title: Admin Feature Delivery
use_cases.description: Create task, user, order, or workflow screens using standard ng-alain table and form patterns.

use_cases.title: Design System Consistency
use_cases.description: Align enterprise pages with shared @delon components, ng-zorro controls, theme variables, and responsive grids.

use_cases.title: Permission-Aware Interfaces
use_cases.description: Add ACL-controlled actions and page elements that match ng-alain authorization conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Table
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create an ng-alain Simple Table component for a list of customers with status badges, paging, and edit actions.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Schema Form
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to build an @delon Schema Form for project creation with validation, async owner selection, and a submit output.

prompt_templates.title: Compose an Admin Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create an Angular admin page with PageHeader, breadcrumbs, a responsive summary grid, a table, and modal create flow.

prompt_templates.title: Add Permissions and Theming
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to refactor an ng-alain feature so actions use @delon ACL, layouts support dark mode, and controls include accessible labels.

output_examples.input: Create a task management table for an ng-alain app.
output_examples.output: A standalone Angular component with ST columns, status badges, action buttons, loading state, paging configuration, and change handlers.

output_examples.input: Add a create task form with ng-alain Schema Form.
output_examples.output: A schema-driven form plan with required fields, select options, textarea configuration, date input, async assignee loading, and submit output behavior.

output_examples.input: Design a responsive enterprise dashboard page.
output_examples.output: A PageHeader-based layout with breadcrumbs, summary statistics, responsive grid sizing, themed cards, and accessibility reminders.

best_practices: Use shared imports and established @delon components before adding custom wrappers.
Replace placeholder logging with real handlers, guarded diagnostics, or telemetry that redacts sensitive values.
Check responsive behavior, dark mode, loading states, and ARIA labels before shipping UI changes.
anti_patterns: Do not copy example console logging into production handlers that process user data.
Do not bypass @delon ACL checks with only hidden buttons or client-side visual restrictions.
Do not mix unrelated UI libraries when ng-alain and ng-zorro already provide the required controls.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps build Angular enterprise components using ng-alain, @delon, and ng-zorro-antd patterns.

faq.question: Does it create complete backend integrations?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on UI components and expects project services and API contracts to already exist.

faq.question: Can it help with access control?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes @delon ACL patterns for permission checks in templates and component code.

faq.question: Does it support responsive layouts?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows ng-zorro grid settings for mobile, tablet, desktop, and large desktop layouts.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found no malicious behavior, but example console logs should be removed in production code.

faq.question: Which assistants can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
